key_risks: Crowded momentum in semis/tech could sharply reverse on any AI-capex or earnings disappointment before exit.
June 16-17 FOMC and June 10 CPI could spark a hawkish repricing hurting high-beta growth and Taiwan.
Sticky inflation (CPI/PCE 3.8%) and falling consumer sentiment may pressure equities broadly.
Geopolitical or currency shock to Taiwan exposure could create concentrated drawdown.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest momentum group; SIA reports Q1 global chip sales +25% QoQ, AI capex set to rise 75% in 2026, and SMH leads trailing returns with broad-based industry strength.
XLK riding AI infrastructure spend ($2.59T forecast 2026); strong 30d momentum with cooling input prices and resilient large-cap earnings.
Direct semiconductor supply-chain leverage with very strong momentum; benefits from same AI/chip cycle and global electronics demand.
Core benchmark exposure to control relative tracking risk while broad earnings growth (~9.4% Q2) and supportive financial conditions persist.
Hedge against sticky inflation (CPI 3.8% YoY, PCE 3.8%) and weakening consumer sentiment; diversifier if growth disappoints into FOMC.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward the dominant AI/semiconductor momentum cycle backed by concrete spending and sales data, anchored with SP500 core exposure and a gold hedge against sticky inflation and a soft consumer.
rationale_summary: Overweight semis and tech leveraging strong AI capex and chip-sales momentum, with SP500 ballast and gold as an inflation/risk hedge.
